title: Bulk report - Week 32
summary:
Capesize
A strong end to the week for the big ships despite a Singapore holiday mid-week and Japan today. The momentum gathered pace when Singapore was on holiday with rates pushing in the Atlantic both for Brazil cargoes and transatlantic and front-haul further north. Today saw rates for C3 Tubarao/Qingdao climb to US$15.30 for early September 170,000-tonne 10% cargoes.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: A strong end to the week for the big ships despite a Singapore holiday mid-week and Japan today. The momentum gathered pace when Singapore was on holiday with rates pushing in the Atlantic both for Brazil cargoes and transatlantic and front-haul further north.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: A busy week here too, despite the holidays with rates firmer across the regions. North Atlantic tonnage remained tight, but the emphasis here was on the shorter Baltic runs with an 81,000-tonner fixing at US$13,000 daily from Hamburg for a trip via the Baltic with redelivery Skaw-Gibraltar.

children.title: Supramax
children.summary: The week started on a quiet note with route levels in negative territory but later some resistance was seen particularly from the US Gulf where activity increased. Limited period activity but a 52,000-dwt 2004-built vessel open east Mediterranean-Black Sea area was fixed for five to seven months trading redelivery worldwide at close to US$10,500 daily.

children.title: Handysize
children.summary: The Asian holidays during this week did nothing to help, as the market remained uneventful. The Handysize Index was still on a gentle downward slope and no period activity reported.
